lines.en_lit: “The wicked are estranged from the womb” Shabbetai the hoarder of fruits came out of her.

ill one feeds him according to experts. Rabbi Yannai said: an ill person says needs and a doctor says he does not need one listens to the ill person. What is the reason “The heart knows the bitterness of its soul” obvious lest you say the doctor is more certain teaches us
a doctor says needs but the ill person says he does not need one listens to the doctor. What is the reason confusion [tunba] has taken hold
We learned ill, one feeds him according to experts. according to experts, yes, his own no, according to experts, yes, according to one expert, no,
With what are we dealing here? says I do not need But let them feed him according to one expert, No, necessary where there is another, with him who says does not need one feeds according to experts

obvious, it is uncertainty life-threatening uncertainty life-threatening is lenient. No, necessary where there are two other with say he does not need And although Rav Safra said two are like one hundred and one hundred are like two that rule applies to the matter of testimony; however, matter of assessing we follow opinions.
However, this applies to assessing monetary but here uncertainty life-threatening

But from that it is taught the latter clause if there are no experts present one feeds him according to his own opinion, by inference, the first clause the ill person said he needs is incomplete and is teaching the following: In what is this statement said? said: I do not need But he said: I do need instead of two experts there is one who says he does not need one feeds him according to his own opinion.
Mar bar Rav Ashi said: Any where says: I need even if there are one hundred who say he does not need we listen to his own as it is stated: “The heart knows the bitterness of its soul”

We learned If there are no experts present, one feeds him according to his own opinion. the reason is there are no experts there were experts no, This is is saying: In what is this statement said? when said: I do not need However, he said: I do need there were no experts there at all; we feed him based on his opinion, as it is stated: “The heart knows the bitterness of its soul”
MISHNA: one who is seized bulmos, one feed him even impure foods until his eyes recover, one whom a mad dog bit, one not feed him from the lobe of liver. And Rabbi Matya ben Ḥarash permits

And furthermore, Rabbi Matya ben Ḥarash said: one who suffers pain in his throat, one place medicine inside his mouth on Shabbat, because uncertainty life-threatening And uncertainty life-threatening overrides Shabbat.

one upon whom a rockslide fell, uncertainty he is there he is not there; and uncertainty alive dead; and uncertainty a gentile a Jew, one clears the pile atop him. they found him alive they clear And if dead, they leave him,
GEMARA: The Sages taught: From where would they know that his eyes had recovered from when he can discern between good and bad Abaye said: with tasting.

The Sages taught: one who is seized bulmos one feeds him the least untithed produce an unslaughtered animal carcass [neveila] one feeds him neveila, untithed produce and Sabbatical Year, Sabbatical Year.

untithed produce and teruma, is tanna’im as it was taught One feeds him untithed produce and does not feed him teruma. Ben Teima says: teruma and not untithed produce. Rabba said: Where it is possible him non-sacred food everyone agrees that one should make fit and feed to him,

Where they disagree, where it is impossible non-sacred food One Sage holds untithed produce is more severe; and one Sage holds teruma is more severe. Sage holds that untithed produce is severe but teruma is fit for a priest, And one Sage holds that teruma is severe while untithed produce can be made fit
